tasks.description: Playbook mode — distraction-free runbook execution view. New page nested at /w/[workspaceId]/runbooks/[runbookId]/playbook showing one step at a time, large/centered, with Mark Complete & Next, prev/next navigation, and progress indicator. Add Playbook Mode button to existing runbook-view header. Also replace window.confirm() with Radix Dialog in runbook-view.tsx (IMP-012).
acceptance_criteria: New server page at src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/runbooks/[runbookId]/playbook/page.tsx fetching runbook + steps from Supabase
PlaybookView client component renders full-viewport overlay (position fixed, z-50+) covering workspace shell — distraction-free with no sidebar/header/tab bar visible
Current step shown large/centered with step name and status
'Mark Complete & Next' primary button sets current step to completed and auto-advances to next pending/in_progress step
Progress indicator: 'Step N of M' text + progress bar
Prev/Next navigation buttons to browse steps without changing status
Completed steps show green checkmark with completion timestamp
Back/Exit button to return to full runbook view (/w/{workspaceId}/runbooks/{runbookId})
Mobile-responsive single-column layout with large touch targets
'Playbook Mode' button added to runbook-view.tsx header next to Complete/Cancel (Link to ./playbook)
window.confirm() in handleCompleteRunbook and handleCancelRunbook replaced with Radix Dialog (IMP-012)
Complete dialog: consequence text explaining completion locks steps
Cancel dialog: consequence text explaining cancellation stops the runbook

similar_files: src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/runbooks/[runbookId]/runbook-view.tsx — reuse RunbookStepEnriched type export, STEP_STATUS_CONFIG, RUNBOOK_STATUS_CONFIG, status class mappings, handleStepStatus pattern, formatDate util
src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/runbooks/[runbookId]/page.tsx — server component pattern: fetch runbook+steps from Supabase, pass to client View component
patterns_to_follow: Server page.tsx: createClient() → supabase.auth.getUser() → fetch runbook+steps → notFound() if missing → render View
Client View: useState for local state, optimistic updates with rollback on API error
Badge, Button from @/components/ui/ with variant props
Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogFooter from @/components/ui/dialog
ArrowLeft, Play, ChevronLeft, ChevronRight icons from lucide-react
learnings_relevant: React 19 useRef requires initial value: useRef(null) not bare useRef()
apiFetch unwraps envelope — returns T directly, no nested property access
Lucide-react has no title prop — use aria-label instead

tasks.special_instructions:
ATTEMPT 3 — previous 2 attempts produced working code but were lost to pipeline merge failures (not code issues). Same approach is valid.

PLAYBOOK VIEW ARCHITECTURE:
- The playbook page is NESTED under runbooks/[runbookId]/playbook/ so it inherits the workspace shell layout. To achieve distraction-free display, PlaybookView must render as a FIXED full-viewport overlay (position: fixed, inset: 0, z-index: 50+, bg-[var(--surface)]) that covers the shell.
- page.tsx follows the same Supabase fetch pattern as the sibling runbook page.tsx — copies its exact queries.
- PlaybookView receives runbook + steps as props (same types as RunbookView).
- Can import and reuse RunbookStepEnriched type from '../runbook-view'.

STEP NAVIGATION:
- useState<number>(0) for currentStepIndex
- Show steps[currentStepIndex] large/centered
- Prev button: decrement index (disabled at 0)
- Next button: increment index (disabled at last)
- 'Mark Complete & Next': call updateRunbookStep(step.id, { status: 'completed', completed_at: now }), optimistic update, then auto-advance to next non-completed step

RUNBOOK-VIEW CHANGES:
- Add 'Playbook' button in header next to Complete/Cancel using Link to './playbook'
- Replace both window.confirm() calls with controlled Radix Dialog state
- Use two boolean states: confirmCompleteOpen, confirmCancelOpen
- Each Dialog has DialogTitle, DialogDescription (consequence sentence), Cancel + Confirm buttons

tasks.description: Add segmented progress bar to runbook list cards — visually distinguish completed (teal) vs in-progress (blue) steps
acceptance_criteria: Each runbook card in runbooks-list-view.tsx shows a small progress bar below the step count text
Bar has two segments: teal/brand for completed steps, lighter blue/signal for in_progress steps
Segment widths are proportional: completedPct = completed/total*100, inProgressPct = inProgress/total*100
Empty state (0 completed, 0 in_progress): gray background bar only
All-completed state: full teal bar
Bar height matches runbook-view pattern (h-1.5), with rounded-full
No layout shift — bar integrates cleanly into existing card layout

similar_files: src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/runbooks/[runbookId]/runbook-view.tsx lines 151-157 — single-segment progress bar pattern (outer bg-[var(--bg-surface-active)] rounded-full, inner bg-[var(--brand)])
patterns_to_follow: Progress bar: <div class='h-1.5 bg-[var(--bg-surface-active)] rounded-full overflow-hidden flex'> with inner divs
In-progress segment: bg-[var(--signal)] (blue) or bg-[var(--accent-blue)] with lower opacity
RunbookStepStatus enum: pending | in_progress | completed | skipped
Data available: runbook.runbook_steps is { id, status }[]

shared_files.note: No shared file modifications needed. Both tasks are UI-only with zero file overlap.
